title: [Bug]: A bug in the use of Connector
description:
<!-- A clear and concise description of bug. -->
When I was testing I found something that confused me.

When I set the URL with rewriteBatchedStatements=true, it produced the following results:
* 1 ** 0 ** 0 *
* 2 ** 1 ** 1 *
* 3 ** 1 ** 1 *
* 5 ** 1 ** 1 *
* 7 ** 1 ** 1 *

But when rewriteBatchedStatements=false, the following results are produced:
* 1 ** 0 ** 0 *
* 2 ** 1 ** 1 *
* 3 ** 1 ** 1 *
* 5 ** 1 ** 1 *
* 7 ** 1 ** 1 *
* 101 ** 1 ** 1 *
* 103 ** 1 ** 1 *

I don't think this makes sense, when using OceanBase Connector/J, enabling rewriteBatchedStatements=true causes some of the inserted data to be lost, while when disabled (rewriteBatchedStatements=false) the inserted data is correct.This issue occurs during bulk inserts and only affects certain records.

This bug also exists in MYSQL, and I also submitted this bug to the MYSQL development team and got it Verified by them.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, value1 BOOLEAN, value2 BOOLEAN, value3 VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'default_value');
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES (1, FALSE, TRUE, 'first');
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES (2, TRUE, NULL, 'second');
SELECT id, value1, value2 FROM test_table WHERE value3 IS NOT NULL;
key_bug_features: rewriteBatchedStatements=true causes data loss during bulk inserts
